raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing three unauthorized charges on my Citi credit card ( ending in XXXX ) : {$310.00} at Macys on XX/XX/XXXX {$54.00} at XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX {$160.00} at XXXX XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX I have not used this card since around XXXX. We have a separate credit account for Macys and have never used our Citi card there. I have also never been to or made any purchases from XXXX XXXX XXXX. Despite promptly responding to a Citi fraud alert stating I did not authorize the transaction, the charges were processed and the card was not blocked. 

I provided everything that Citi requested, but Citi informed me that the documentation was insufficient and told me to contact the merchants myself. I find this response deeply disappointing, especially as a customer with over 20 years of history with Citi, holding not only a credit account but also checking, savings, and CD accounts. 

Under the Fair Credit Billing Act, I should not be held liable for these charges, and I am requesting CFPB 's assistance in getting a full refund.
